title: Staggered and tile stacked microdevice integration and driving
abstract: What is disclosed is structures and methods to integrate microdevices into system or receiver substrates. The integration of microdevices is facilitated by adding staging pads to microdevices before or after transferring. Creating stages after the transfer of a first microdevice to a substrate for the subsequent microdevice transfer to the first (or the second) microdevice transfer. The stage improves the surface profile of the substrate so that next microdevice can be transferred without the first microdevice on the substrate get damaged by or interfere with the surface of the donor or transfer head. Some embodiments further relate to tiled display device and more particularly, to stacking tiles to a backplane to form the tiled display device.
claims:
Claims 1. A method of integrating microdevices, the method comprising: providing one or more microdevices on a donor substrate; integrating a first microdevice from the donor substrate to a system substrate; providing one or more staging pads to a second microdevice on the donor substrate to raise a location of the second microdevice; and integrating the second microdevice to the system substrate. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more staging pads are one of: a conductive layer, a planarization layer, or a pillar formed on a substrate or microdevice surface. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more staging pads create a connection between the microdevices and the system substrate. 4. The method of claim 1, further comprising providing one or more planarization layers over integrated microdevices to the system substrate. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein an opening is created in the planarization layer to couple the microdevices to a signal or the system substrate. 6. The method of claim 1, further comprising depositing an electrode over the microdevices through the opening in the planarization layer. 7. The method of claim 1, further comprising adding a bonding layer to the electrode and the microdevices. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein a reflective layer is provided to each microdevice. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the first microdevice and the second microdevice are overlapped and connected through a common electrode. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein one or more programmable switches are provided to couple the electrode to each microdevice. 11. A method of stacking tile structure layers to a substrate, wherein each tile structure layer comprises a plurality of microdevices, the method comprising: releasing a first tile structure layer on a substrate; providing and aligning a second tile structure layer with the first tile structure layer on the substrate; and bonding the first tile structure layer and the second tile structure layer on the substrate, wherein the first tile structure and the second tile structure are physically separated from each other before bonding or they are processed separately prior to transfer. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the second tile structure layer is aligned with the first tile structure layer through alignment marks. 13. The method of claim 11, wherein the second tile structure layer is bonded to the first tile structure layer through means of an adhesive layer or bonding pads. 14. The method of claim 11, wherein the first tile structure layer and second tile structure layer is covered with planarization layers and metallization layers. 15. The method of claim 14, further comprising providing vias through the planarization layers to connect first tile structure layer and second tile structure layer together or provide connection to connect different tile structure layers to the substrate. 16. The method of claim 11, further comprising providing vias through the first tile structure layer and the second tile structure layer to couple the tile structure layers to controlling and biasing signals. 17. The method of claim 11 , wherein the substrate is one of: a temporary substrate, a system substrate or a backplane. 18. The method of claim 11, wherein the substrate is affixed to either a bottom surface, a top surface or in between the tile structure layers. 19. The method of claim 11, wherein the microdevices are of the same or different colors. 20. The method of claim 11, further comprising providing driving circuitry on the substrate prior to stacking the tile layer structures. 21. The method of claim 11, further comprising testing each tile structure layer prior to stacking. 22. The method of claim 11, further comprising providing bonding layers between the tile structures layers for enabling integration of microdevices. 23. The method of claim 11, further comprising providing common electrode between each tile structure layer for connecting at least one terminal of the microdevices together. 24. The method of claim 11, further comprising forming an optical structure on each microdevice at different tile structure layers. 25. The method of claim 11, wherein the bonding layers is one of adhesive or bonding pads. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the adhesive is one of: SU8, Polyamide, UV or thermal curable epoxy. 27. The method of claim 25, wherein the bonding pads is one of: Au/In, Au/Au or other materials compatible with compression, thermal compression, and eutectic bonding methods. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the bonding pads formed on either side of the microdevice between the tile structures layers. 29. The method of claim 11 wherein the second tile structure layer has dents in the planarization layer to receive microdevices from the first tile layer structure. 30. The method of claim 11, wherein an excess of planarization layer around the microdevices in the first layer structure is removed to ease the bonding. 31. A display device comprising: 

 a plurality of tile structures stacked with each other wherein each tile structure comprises: a substrate; an array of microdevices disposed on the substrate; top electrodes disposed on a first surface of each microdevice; bottom electrodes disposed on a surface opposite to the first surface of the microdevices; and a backplane bonded to the plurality of the tile structures. 32. The display device of claim 31, wherein the plurality of tile structures are transferred to a temporary substrate before bonding with the backplane. 33. The display device of claim 31, wherein the plurality of tile structures are bonded with each other through a bonding layer in between. 34. The display device of claim 31, wherein a planarization layer is formed between the array of microdevices for each tile structure. 35. The display device of claim 31, wherein the planarization layer provides openings for the top electrodes and the bottom electrodes to make connections with microdevices. 36. The display device of claim 31, wherein a common electrode is provided between each tile structures for connecting the microdevices together. 37. The display device of claim 31, wherein the bottom electrodes comprise bonding pads for the microdevices to bond with the backplane. 38. The display device of claim 31, wherein the backplane comprises driving circuitry and contact pads corresponding to the bonding pads of the microdevices. 39. The display device of claim 31, wherein an optical layer is provided on the temporary substrate prior to stacking tile structures to the temporary substrate. 40. The display device of claim 31, wherein the stacked tiles structures are patterned to create islands at the temporary substrate or after bonding to the backplane. 41. The display device of claim 31, wherein a reflective layer is provided around the islands to reduce a viewing angle effect by directing light from different tile structures to one surface. 42. The display device of claim 31, wherein the temporary substrate is removed after bonding with the backplane. 43. The display device of claim 31, wherein a space between the microdevices is filled by a filling material before or after bonding with the backplane. 44. A solid state array device comprising one or more pixel wherein each pixel has two or more microdevices and at least one of the two microdevices is coupled to a pixel circuit and each microdevice is coupled to a bias voltage through a corresponding switch. 45. The solid state array device of claim 44, wherein the pixel circuit is one of: a pixel circuit corresponding to each microdevice and a shared pixel circuit between the microdevices. 46. The solid state array device of claim 45, wherein the shared pixel circuit is allocated to each microdevice for a given time during a frame time. 47. The solid state array device of claim 45, wherein the one of the microdevices is coupled to the shared pixel circuit through the switch and decoupled from the bias voltage, to turn on the microdevice during an operational mode. 48. The solid state array device of claim 45, wherein the one of the microdevices is decoupled from the shared pixel circuit through the switch and coupled to the bias voltage, to turn off the microdevice during an un-operational mode. 49. The solid state array device of claim 45, wherein an on time of each microdevice is extended and at least two microdevices are operational during one sub frame of a frame time. 50. The solid state array device of claim 45, wherein each microdevice is provided with a corresponding storage element coupled to the shared pixel circuit through corresponding switches. 51. The solid state array device of claim 50, wherein the storage element comprises a capacitor. 52. A multi-color structure comprising of stacked microdevices: a first microdevice bonded on a first side of the first microdevice surface via a first bonding layer of the first microdevice on a stacking substrate; and a second microdevice bonded via a first bonding layer of the second microdevice to a second bonding layer of the first microdevice on a second side of the first microdevice. 53. The multi-color structure of claim 52, wherein, at least a third microdevice is bonded via the first bonding layer of the third microdevice to a second bonding layer of the second microdevice. 54. The multi-color structure of claim 52, wherein, at least one side of the stacked microdevices has a stair shaped structure to provide access to an interface between stacked microdevices. 55. The multi-color structure of claim 54, wherein, edges of stacked microdevices are covered by a dielectric. 56. The multi-color structure of claim 52, wherein access to an interface between two stacked microdevices is provided through a VIA. 57. The multi-color structure of claim 56, where the VIA sidewalls are isolated by a dielectric. 58. The multi-color structure of claim 52, wherein the first and second bonding layers of microdevices are transparent. 59. The multi-color structure of claim 58, where the bonding layers are patterned for light to pass through the micro device stack. 60. The multi-color structure of claim 59, where the interface between microdevices is partly filled with filler layers.
